name: eval-2-high-judgment-keep-human
query: Should I automate writing performance reviews for my direct reports? I write 8 of them twice a year.
expected_behavior: Evaluates against the four criteria: repetitiveness (16/year = medium), judgment required (very high — personalized, high-stakes, requires context the automation cannot have), error tolerance (very low — mistakes damage relationships and careers), data sensitivity (high — HR-confidential)
Produces recommendation: Keep Human with AI-assist for specific sub-tasks

Specifically flags which sub-tasks CAN be AI-assisted: drafting boilerplate sections, ensuring consistent tone across reviews, spell-checking, surfacing patterns in feedback — none of which involve the human judgment core
Specifically flags what MUST stay human: evaluating performance, choosing examples, rating, determining growth areas, the final review itself
Does not recommend full automation — high judgment + low error tolerance + high sensitivity is the textbook 'keep human' zone
Surfaces the risk of full automation clearly: legal exposure, employee trust, loss of managerial signal
failure_modes_to_check: Recommends full automation because it's repetitive
Recommends keep-human without identifying any AI-assist opportunities (misses the hybrid path)
